core_model.structure_summary: An identity stating that the expectation of an exponential functional over a probability measure on trajectories equals the ratio of two normalizing constants of exponential (Gibbs-type) weights, derived by maximizing an entropy functional over path space subject to constraints.
core_model.canonical_form: \left\langle e^{-\beta W[\gamma]}\right\rangle = \frac{Z_B}{Z_A} = e^{-\beta\Delta F}, \qquad P[\gamma] \propto e^{-\beta H(\gamma)},\; Z=\int e^{-\beta H}\,d\gamma

variables.meaning: trajectory/path in a state space, weighted by a probability measure
entropy of the path distribution being extremized under constraints
inverse-scale weighting constant in the exponential Gibbs measure
path-ensemble randomness over which the exponential functional is averaged

notes: The paper is a derivation of an exponential-average identity (Jarzynski-type) from a maximum-path-entropy / maximum-caliber variational principle. Two defensible object_type choices: 'statistical_model' (inference over path space, chosen here) or 'variational' (the max-entropy extremization). The load-bearing structure is the equality of an ensemble exponential average to a ratio of partition functions, plus the inference/Bayesian ratio equations. Equations are conceptual identities rather than a single dynamical equation.
